config.anti_entropy.enabled,\n        };\n\n        let migration_coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(\n            MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config.clone())\n        ));\n\n        // Create migration executor for actual HTTP document migration\n        use miroir_core::rebalancer::HttpMigrationExecutor;\n        let migration_executor = Arc::new(HttpMigrationExecutor::new(\n            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n        ));\n\n        let rebalancer = Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            rebalancer_config.clone(),\n            topology_arc.clone(),\n            migration_config.clone(),\n        ).with_migration_executor(migration_executor));\n\n        // Create rebalancer metrics\n        let rebalancer_metrics = Arc::new(RwLock::new(RebalancerMetrics::default()));\n\n        // Get or create task store for rebalancer worker\n        let task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>> = match config.task_store.backend.as_str() {\n            \"redis\" => {\n                redis_store.as_ref().map(|s| Arc::new(s.clone()) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>)\n            }\n            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n                Some(Arc::new(miroir_core::task_store::SqliteTaskStore::open(\n                    std::path::Path::new(&config.task_store.path)\n                ).expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")) as Arc<dyn TaskStore>)\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        };\n\n        // Create rebalancer worker if task store is available\n        let rebalancer_worker = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n            let worker_config = RebalancerWorkerConfig {\n                max_concurrent_migrations: config.rebalancer.max_concurrent_migrations,\n                lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n                lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n                migration_batch_size: 1000,\n                migration_batch_delay_ms: 100,\n                event_channel_capacity: 100,\n            };\n\n            // Create metrics callback for rebalancer operations\n            let metrics_for_worker = metrics.clone();\n            let rebalancer_metrics_callback: RebalancerMetricsCallback = Arc::new(\n                move |in_progress: bool, docs_migrated: Option<u64>, duration_secs: Option<f64>| {\n                    if in_progress {\n                        metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(true);\n                    } else {\n                        metrics_for_worker.set_rebalance_in_progress(false);\n                    }\n                    if let Some(count) = docs_migrated {\n                        metrics_for_worker.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(count);\n                    }\n                    if let Some(duration) = duration_secs {\n                        metrics_for_worker.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n                    }\n                }\n            );\n\n            Some(Arc::new(RebalancerWorker::with_metrics(\n                worker_config,\n                topology_arc.clone(),\n                store.clone(),\n                rebalancer.clone(),\n                migration_coordinator.clone(),\n                rebalancer_metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id.clone(),\n                Some(rebalancer_metrics_callback),\n            )))\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Create settings broadcast coordinator (§13.5)\n        let settings_broadcast = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n            Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::with_task_store(store.clone()))\n        } else {\n            Arc::new(miroir_core::settings::SettingsBroadcast::new())\n        };\n\n        // Create drift reconciler worker (§13.5) if task store is available\n        let drift_reconciler = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n            let node_addresses = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n            let drift_config = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::DriftReconcilerConfig {\n                interval_s: config.settings_drift_check.interval_s,\n                auto_repair: config.settings_drift_check.auto_repair,\n                lease_ttl_secs: 10,\n                lease_renewal_interval_ms: 2000,\n            };\n            Some(Arc::new(miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::DriftReconciler::new(\n                drift_config,\n                settings_broadcast.clone(),\n                store.clone(),\n                node_addresses,\n                config.node_master_key.clone(),\n                pod_id.clone(),\n            )))\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Create anti-entropy worker (plan §13.8) if task store is available\n        let anti_entropy_worker = if config.anti_entropy.enabled {\n            if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n                let ae_worker_config = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::AntiEntropyWorkerConfig::from_schedule(\n                    &config.anti_entropy.schedule\n                );\n                let metrics_for_ae_1 = metrics.clone();\n                let metrics_for_ae_2 = metrics.clone();\n                let metrics_for_ae_3 = metrics.clone();\n                let metrics_for_ae_4 = metrics.clone();\n                let mut ae_worker = miroir_core::rebalancer_worker::AntiEntropyWorker::new(\n                    ae_worker_config,\n                    topology_arc.clone(),\n                    store.clone(),\n                    config.node_master_key.clone(),\n                    pod_id.clone(),\n                );\n                // Wire up metrics callbacks\n                ae_worker = ae_worker.with_metrics(\n                    Arc::new(move |count: u64| {\n                        metrics_for_ae_1.inc_antientropy_shards_scanned(count);\n                    }),\n                    Arc::new(move |count: u64| {\n                        metrics_for_ae_2.inc_antientropy_mismatches_found(count);\n                    }),\n                    Arc::new(move |count: u64| {\n                        metrics_for_ae_3.inc_antientropy_docs_repaired(count);\n                    }),\n                    Arc::new(move |timestamp: u64| {\n                        metrics_for_ae_4.set_antientropy_last_scan_completed(timestamp);\n                    }),\n                );\n                // Set TTL enabled flag from config\n                ae_worker.set_ttl_enabled(config.anti_entropy.ttl_enabled);\n                Some(Arc::new(ae_worker))\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Create session pinning manager (§13.6)\n        let session_manager = Arc::new(miroir_core::session_pinning::SessionManager::new(\n            miroir_core::session_pinning::SessionPinningConfig::from(\n                config.session_pinning.clone()\n            ),\n        ));\n\n        // Create alias registry (§13.7)\n        // Note: Aliases are loaded asynchronously in background, not during initialization\n        let alias_registry = Arc::new(miroir_core::alias::AliasRegistry::new());\n\n        // Create leader election service (plan §14.5) if task store is available\n        let leader_election = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n            // Create metrics callback for leader election\n            let metrics_for_leader = metrics.clone();\n            let metrics_callback: LeaderElectionMetricsCallback = Arc::new(\n                move |metric_name: &str, labels: &std::collections::HashMap<String, String>, value: f64| {\n                    if metric_name == \"miroir_leader\" {\n                        if let Some(scope) = labels.get(\"scope\") {\n                            metrics_for_leader.set_leader(scope, value > 0.0);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            );\n\n            let leader_config = config.leader_election.clone();\n            let mut leader = LeaderElection::new(\n                store.clone(),\n                pod_id.clone(),\n                leader_config,\n            );\n            leader = leader.with_metrics_callback(metrics_callback);\n            Some(Arc::new(leader))\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        // Create Mode C worker for chunked background jobs (plan §14.5 Mode C)\n        let mode_c_worker = if let Some(ref store) = task_store {\n            let worker_config = ModeCWorkerConfig {\n                poll_interval_ms: 1000,      // 1 second\n                heartbeat_interval_ms: 10000, // 10 seconds\n                max_concurrent_jobs: 3,\n            };\n            Some(Arc::new(ModeCWorker::new(\n                store.clone(),\n                pod_id.clone(),\n                worker_config,\n            )))\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        Self {\n            config: Arc::new(config),\n            topology: topology_arc,\n            ready: Arc::new(RwLock::new(false)),\n            metrics,\n            version_state,\n            task_registry: Arc::new(task_registry),\n            redis_store,\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            seal_key,\n            local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter::new(),\n            local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter::new(),\n            rebalancer: Some(rebalancer),\n            migration_coordinator: Some(migration_coordinator),\n            rebalancer_worker,\n            rebalancer_metrics,\n            previous_docs_migrated: Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n            settings_broadcast,\n            drift_reconciler,\n            anti_entropy_worker,\n            session_manager,\n            alias_registry,\n            leader_election,\n            mode_c_worker,\n            replica_selector: Arc::new(miroir_core::replica_selection::ReplicaSelector::default()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n\n        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n            if healthy.len() < required {\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        true\n    }\n\n    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n    pub async fn sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus(&self) {\n        if let Some(ref rebalancer) = self.rebalancer {\n            let rebalancer_metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n            let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n\n            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n            if current_total > previous {\n                let delta = current_total - previous;\n                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n            }\n\n            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n            if duration > 0.0 {\n                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TopologyResponse {\n    pub shards: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: u32,\n    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,\n    pub degraded_node_count: u32,\n    pub rebalance_in_progress: bool,\n    pub fully_covered: bool,\n}\n\n/// Per-node information in the topology response.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeInfo {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub address: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub shard_count: u32,\n    pub last_seen_ms: u64,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardsResponse {\n    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\npub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n\n    // Count degraded nodes\n    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n\n    // Check rebalance status\n    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        status.in_progress\n    } else {\n        false\n    };\n\n    // Build node info list\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n        .nodes()\n        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n            id: n.id.as_str().to_string(),\n            address: n.address.clone(),\n            status: format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Check if fully covered\n    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n\n    let response = TopologyResponse {\n        shards: topo.shards,\n        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n        nodes,\n        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n        rebalance_in_progress,\n        fully_covered,\n    };\n\n    Ok(Json(response))\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\npub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n            for node_id in assigned {\n                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\npub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n\n    if ready {\n        Ok(\"\")\n    } else {\n        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n        if has_quorum {\n            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n            Ok(\"\")\n        } else {\n            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\npub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n///\n/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\npub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n        redis,\n        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n    };\n\n    info!(\n        index = %index,\n        force = body.force,\n        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n    );\n\n    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\npub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 2 {\n        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n    }\n    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n    };\n    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n}\n\n/// Generate a random session ID.\nfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n    hex::encode(&bytes)\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// ```json\n/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n/// ```\n///\n/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n/// ```json\n/// { \"success\": true }\n/// ```\n///\n/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n///\n/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\npub async fn admin_login<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n) -> Response\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Parse rate limit config\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n            return (\n                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n                }),\n            ).into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Check rate limit and backoff\n    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n                            warn!(\n                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                                wait_seconds = ws,\n                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n                            );\n                            return (\n                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                                    success: false,\n                                    message: Some(format!(\n                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n                                        ws\n                                    )),\n                                }),\n                            ).into_response();\n                        } else {\n                            return (\n                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                                    success: false,\n                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n                                }),\n                            ).into_response();\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        // Local backend rate limiting\n        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return (\n                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n                        Some(format!(\n                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n                            ws\n                        ))\n                    } else {\n                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n                    },\n                }),\n            ).into_response();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n        if backend == \"redis\" {\n            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n                }\n            }\n        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n        }\n\n        // Generate session ID and seal it\n        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n                return (\n                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                        success: false,\n                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n                    }),\n                ).into_response();\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n            \"admin login successful\"\n        );\n\n        // Set cookie and return success\n        (\n            StatusCode::OK,\n            [\n                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n            ],\n            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                success: true,\n                message: None,\n            }),\n        ).into_response()\n    } else {\n        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n        warn!(\n            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n        );\n\n        if backend == \"redis\" {\n            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n\n                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n                    &source_ip,\n                    failed_threshold,\n                    backoff_start_minutes,\n                    backoff_max_hours,\n                ) {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n                }\n            }\n        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n\n            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n                &source_ip,\n                failed_threshold,\n                backoff_start_minutes,\n                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n            );\n        }\n\n        (\n            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                success: false,\n                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n            }),\n        ).into_response()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// ```json\n/// {\n///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n///   \"replica_group\": 0\n/// }\n/// ```\n///\n/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\npub async fn add_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n        .to_string();\n\n    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n        .to_string();\n\n    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n        as u32;\n\n    // Add node to topology\n    {\n        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n        // Check if node already exists\n        let node_id = NodeId::new(id.clone());\n        if topo.node(&node_id).is_some() {\n            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n        }\n        // Check if replica group exists\n        let group_count = topo.groups().count() as u32;\n        if replica_group >= group_count {\n            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n                format!(\"Replica group {} does not exist\", replica_group)));\n        }\n        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n        topo.add_node(node);\n    }\n\n    // Send event to rebalancer worker (if available)\n    if let Some(ref worker) = app_state.rebalancer_worker {\n        let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n            node_id: id.clone(),\n            replica_group,\n            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n        };\n        if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n        }\n    }\n\n    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"node_id\": id,\n        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} added to replica group {}, rebalancing will start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n    })))\n}\n\n/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n///\n/// Request body (optional):\n/// ```json\n/// {\n///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n/// }\n/// ```\n///\n/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\npub async fn remove_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let force = body.get(\"force\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n        .unwrap_or(false);\n\n    let node_id_obj = NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n\n    // Check node state\n    let node_status = {\n        let topo = app_state.topology.read().await;\n        let node = topo.node(&node_id_obj)\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, format!(\"Node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n\n        // Check if this is the last node in the group\n        let group = topo.groups()\n            .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n\n        if group.nodes().len() <= 1 {\n            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Cannot remove the last node in a replica group\".into()));\n        }\n\n        node.status\n    };\n\n    if !force && node_status != miroir_core::topology::NodeStatus::Draining {\n        return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, format!(\n            \"Node {} is not in draining state (current: {:?}), use force=true to bypass\",\n            node_id, node_status\n        ).into()));\n    }\n\n    // Remove node from topology\n    {\n        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n        topo.remove_node(&node_id_obj);\n    }\n\n    info!(node_id = %node_id, force, \"Node removal completed\");\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"node_id\": node_id,\n        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} removed from cluster\", node_id),\n    })))\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain — Drain a node (prepare for removal).\n///\n/// Implements plan §2 node drain flow:\n/// 1. Mark node as `draining`\n/// 2. Send `NodeDraining` event to rebalancer worker\n/// 3. Worker computes shard destinations and starts migration with leader lease\npub async fn drain_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Check if worker is available\n    let worker = app_state.rebalancer_worker.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer worker not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    // Get node info and mark as draining\n    let replica_group = {\n        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n        let node_id_obj = NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n        let node = topo.node(&node_id_obj)\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, format!(\"Node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n\n        // Check if this is the last node in the group\n        let group = topo.groups()\n            .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n\n        if group.nodes().len() <= 1 {\n            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Cannot remove the last node in a replica group\".into()));\n        }\n\n        let replica_group =   replica_group\n    };\n\n    // Send event to rebalancer worker\n    let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeDraining {\n        node_id: node_id.clone(),\n        replica_group,\n        index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n        error!(error = %e, node_id = %node_id, \"failed to send NodeDraining event to rebalancer worker\");\n        return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Failed to queue drain: {}\", e)));\n    }\n\n    info!(node_id = %node_id, replica_group, \"Node drain queued for rebalancing\");\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"node_id\": node_id,\n        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} is draining, migrations will start State(state): State<S>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: unseal.\n34\t/// Handlers extract this to look up the session in the task store.\n35\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n36\tpub struct AdminSessionId(pub String);\n37\t\n38\t/// State for CSRF middleware, Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n46\t// JWT claims (plan §13.21)\n47\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\t/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct JwtClaims {\n52\t    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n53\t    pub sub: String,\n54\t    /// Index this token grants access to.\n55\t    pub idx: String,\n56\t    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n57\t    pub scope: String,\n58\t    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n59\t    pub iat: u64,\n60\t    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n61\t    pub exp: u64,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\t/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\n65\tconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\n66\tconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n67\t\n68\t/// JWT header (always HS256).\n69\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n70\tstruct JwtHeader {\n71\t    alg: String,\n72\t    kid: String,\n73\t    typ: String,\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n77\t// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n78\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n79\t\n80\t/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\n81\tfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n82\t    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n83\t    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n84\t\n85\t    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n86\t    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n87\t\n88\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n89\t\n90\t    let mut mac =\n91\t        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n92\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n93\t    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n94\t    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n95\t\n96\t    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n97\t}\n98\t\n99\t/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\n100\tfn jwt_decode(\n101\t    token: &str,\n102\t    secret: &[u8],\n103\t) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n104\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n105\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n106\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n110\t        .decode(parts[0])\n111\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n112\t    let header: JwtHeader =\n113\t        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n114\t\n115\t    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n116\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    // Verify signature\n120\t    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n121\t    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n122\t    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n123\t    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n124\t\n125\t    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n126\t        .decode(parts[2])\n127\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n128\t\n129\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n130\t    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n131\t    if !sig_valid {\n132\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    // Decode claims\n136\t    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n137\t        .decode(parts[1])\n138\t        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n139\t    let claims: JwtClaims =\n140\t        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n141\t\n142\t    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n143\t    let now = epoch_seconds();\n144\t    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n145\t        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    Ok((header, claims))\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n152\t// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n153\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n154\t\n155\t/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n156\t#[derive(Clone)]\n157\tpub struct AuthState {\n158\t    pub master_key: String,\n159\t    pub admin_key: String,\n160\t    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n161\t    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n162\t    /// Optional previous secret active during rotation overlap window.\n163\t    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,\n164\t    /// Key for sealing/unsealing admin session cookies (XChaCha20-Poly1305).\n165\t    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n166\t    /// In-memory set of revoked admin session IDs (populated on logout, Pub/Sub).\n167\t    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n168\t    /// Counter for revoked admin sessions (miroir_admin_session_revoked_total).\n169\t    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n170\t}\n171\t\n172\timpl std::fmt::Debug for AuthState {\n173\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n174\t        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n175\t            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n176\t            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n177\t            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n178\t            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n179\t            .field(\"seal_key\", &self.seal_key)\n180\t            .field(\"revoked_sessions\", &self.revoked_sessions.len())\n181\t            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n182\t    }\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n186\t// JWT signing / validation helpers\n187\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n188\t\n189\timpl AuthState {\n190\t    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n191\t    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n192\t    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n193\t        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n194\t        let now = epoch_seconds();\n195\t        let claims = JwtClaims {\n196\t            sub: sub.to_string(),\n197\t            idx: idx.to_string(),\n198\t            scope: scope.to_string(),\n199\t            iat: now,\n200\t            exp: now + ttl_s,\n201\t        };\n202\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n203\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n204\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n205\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n206\t        };\n207\t        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n211\t    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n212\t    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n213\t        // Try primary secret first\n214\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n215\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n216\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n217\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n218\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n219\t            }\n220\t        }\n221\t\n222\t        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n223\t        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n224\t            if secret.is_empty() {\n225\t                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n226\t            }\n227\t            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n228\t                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n229\t                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n230\t                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n231\t            }\n232\t        }\n233\t\n234\t        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n235\t    }\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n239\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n240\tpub enum JwtValidationError {\n241\t    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n242\t    Malformed,\n243\t    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n244\t    InvalidSignature,\n245\t    /// Token has expired.\n246\t    Expired,\n247\t    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n248\t    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n249\t}\n250\t\n251\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n252\t// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n253\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n254\t\n255\t/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n256\t/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\n257\tpub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n258\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n259\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n260\t    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\n264\tpub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n265\t    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\n269\tpub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n270\t    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n274\t/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\n275\tpub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n276\t    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n277\t        Ok(())\n278\t    } else {\n279\t        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n280\t    }\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n284\t// Origin validation (plan §9)\n285\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n286\t\n287\t/// Result of origin validation.\n288\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n289\tpub enum OriginVerdict {\n290\t    /// Origin is allowed.\n291\t    Allowed,\n292\t    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n293\t    Forbidden,\n294\t    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n295\t    Missing,\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n299\t/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\n300\tpub fn validate_origin(\n301\t    headers: &HeaderMap,\n302\t    allowed_origins: &[String],\n303\t    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n304\t) -> OriginVerdict {\n305\t    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n306\t    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n307\t\n308\t    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n309\t    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n310\t\n311\t    let provided_origin = match referer {\n312\t        Some(o) => o,\n313\t        None => {\n314\t            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n315\t            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n316\t                OriginVerdict::Missing\n317\t            } else {\n318\t                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n319\t            };\n320\t        }\n321\t    };\n322\t\n323\t    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n324\t    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n325\t        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n326\t            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n327\t            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n328\t                &ref_val[..idx]\n329\t            } else {\n330\t                ref_val\n331\t            }\n332\t        } else {\n333\t            provided_origin\n334\t        }\n335\t    } else {\n336\t        provided_origin\n337\t    };\n338\t\n339\t    // Check against allowed origins\n340\t    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n341\t        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n342\t        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n343\t            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n344\t                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n345\t                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n346\t                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n347\t                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n348\t                }\n349\t            }\n350\t        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n351\t            // Wildcard allows any origin\n352\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n353\t        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n354\t            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n355\t        }\n356\t    }\n357\t\n358\t    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n362\t// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n363\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n364\t\n365\t/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n366\t/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\n367\tpub fn build_csp_header(\n368\t    base_template: &str,\n369\t    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n370\t) -> String {\n371\t    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n372\t        .split(';')\n373\t        .map(|s| s.trim())\n374\t        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n375\t        .map(|directive| {\n376\t            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n377\t            if parts.len() == 2 {\n378\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n379\t            } else {\n380\t                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n381\t            }\n382\t        })\n383\t        .collect();\n384\t\n385\t    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n386\t    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n387\t                      name: &str,\n388\t                      values: &[String]| {\n389\t        if values.is_empty() {\n390\t            return;\n391\t        }\n392\t        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n393\t        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n394\t            // Append to existing directive\n395\t            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n396\t            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n397\t        } else {\n398\t            // Add new directive\n399\t            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n400\t        }\n401\t    };\n402\t\n403\t    // Merge each override category\n404\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n405\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n406\t    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n407\t\n408\t    // Rebuild CSP string\n409\t    directives\n410\t        .into_iter()\n411\t        .map(|(name, values)| {\n412\t            if values.is_empty() {\n413\t                name\n414\t            } else {\n415\t                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n416\t            }\n417\t        })\n418\t        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n419\t        .join(\"; \")\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n423\t// Dispatch verdict\n424\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n425\t\n426\t/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n427\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n428\tpub enum AuthVerdict {\n429\t    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n430\t    Exempt,\n431\t    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n432\t    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n433\t    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n434\t    JwtInvalid,\n435\t    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n436\t    JwtScopeDenied,\n437\t    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n438\t    InvalidAuth,\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n442\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n443\tpub enum TokenKind {\n444\t    MasterKey,\n445\t    AdminKey,\n446\t    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n447\t    Jwt,\n448\t    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n449\t    AdminSession,\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\timpl AuthVerdict {\n453\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n454\t        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n455\t    }\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n459\t// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n460\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n461\t\n462\t/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n463\t/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n464\t/// are never consulted.\n465\tpub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n466\t    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n467\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n468\t        return true;\n469\t    }\n470\t\n471\t    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n472\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n473\t        return true;\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n477\t    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n478\t        return true;\n479\t    }\n480\t\n481\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n482\t    if method == Method::GET {\n483\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n484\t            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n485\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n486\t        }\n487\t    }\n488\t\n489\t    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n490\t    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n491\t        return true;\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n495\t    if method == Method::GET {\n496\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n497\t            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n498\t            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n499\t                return true;\n500\t            }\n501\t        }\n502\t    }\n503\t\n504\t    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n505\t    if method == Method::GET {\n506\t        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n507\t            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n508\t            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n509\t        }\n510\t    }\n511\t\n512\t    false\n513\t}\n514\t\n515\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n516\t// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n517\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n518\t\n519\t/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n520\t/// dot-separated base64url segments).\n521\tpub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n522\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n523\t    if parts.len() != 3 {\n524\t        return false;\n525\t    }\n526\t    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n527\t    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n528\t        !s.is_empty()\n529\t            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n530\t    })\n531\t}\n532\t\n533\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n534\t// Helper — admin path check\n535\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n536\t\n537\t/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\n538\tpub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n539\t    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n540\t}\n541\t\n542\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n543\t// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n544\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n545\t\n546\t/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n547\t/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\n548\tpub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n549\t    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n550\t}\n551\t\n552\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n553\t// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n554\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n555\t\n556\t/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n557\t///\n558\t/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n559\t/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\n560\tpub fn dispatch_bearer(\n561\t    method: &Method,\n562\t    path: &str,\n563\t    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n564\t    state: &AuthState,\n565\t) -> AuthVerdict {\n566\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n567\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n568\t        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    let token = match bearer_token {\n572\t        Some(t) => t,\n573\t        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n574\t    };\n575\t\n576\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n577\t    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n578\t        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n579\t            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n580\t            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n581\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n582\t            }\n583\t            Err(_) => {\n584\t                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n585\t            }\n586\t        }\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n590\t    if is_admin_path(path) {\n591\t        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n592\t            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n593\t        }\n594\t        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n598\t    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n599\t        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n600\t    }\n601\t\n602\t    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n603\t    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n607\t// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n608\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n609\t\n610\t/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n611\t/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n612\t/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\n613\tpub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n614\t    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n615\t        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n616\t        None => false,\n617\t    }\n618\t}\n619\t\n620\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n621\t// Axum middleware\n622\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n623\t\n624\t/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\n625\tfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n626\t    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n627\t    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\n631\tpub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n632\t    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n633\t    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n634\t        let pair = pair.trim();\n635\t        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n636\t            return Some(value.to_string());\n637\t        }\n638\t    }\n639\t    None\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\n643\tpub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n644\t    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n645\t}\n646\t\n647\t/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\n648\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n649\t    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n650\t    req: Request,\n651\t    next: Next,\n652\t) -> Response {\n653\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n654\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n655\t\n656\t    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n657\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n658\t        return next.run(req).await;\n659\t    }\n660\t\n661\t    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n662\t    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n663\t        return next.run(req).await;\n664\t    }\n665\t\n666\t    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n667\t    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n668\t    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n669\t    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n670\t        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n671\t            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n672\t                Ok(session_id) => {\n673\t                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n674\t                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n675\t                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n676\t                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n677\t                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n678\t                        )\n679\t                        .into_response();\n680\t                    }\n681\t                    let mut req = req;\n682\t                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n683\t                    return next.run(req).await;\n684\t                }\n685\t                Err(e) => {\n686\t                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n687\t                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n688\t                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n689\t                    tracing::warn!(\n690\t                        path = %path,\n691\t                        error = %e,\n692\t                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n693\t                    );\n694\t                }\n695\t            }\n696\t        }\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    // Extract bearer token\n700\t    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n701\t\n702\t    // Run the dispatch chain\n703\t    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n704\t\n705\t    match verdict {\n706\t        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n707\t        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n708\t            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n709\t            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n710\t        )\n711\t        .into_response(),\n712\t        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n713\t            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n714\t            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n715\t        )\n716\t        .into_response(),\n717\t        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n718\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n719\t            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n720\t        )\n721\t        .into_response(),\n722\t    }\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n726\t// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n727\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n728\t\n729\t/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n730\t///\n731\t/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n732\t/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n733\t/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n734\t/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n735\t/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n736\t///\n737\t/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n738\t/// the token stored in the session.\n739\tpub async fn csrf_middleware(\n740\t    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n741\t    req: Request,\n742\t    next: Next,\n743\t) -> Response {\n744\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n745\t    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n746\t\n747\t    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n748\t    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n749\t        return next.run(req).await;\n750\t    }\n751\t\n752\t    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n753\t    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n754\t        return next.run(req).await;\n755\t    }\n756\t\n757\t    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n758\t    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n759\t        return next.run(req).await;\n760\t    }\n761\t\n762\t    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n763\t    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n764\t        return next.run(req).await;\n765\t    }\n766\t\n767\t    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n768\t    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n769\t    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n770\t\n771\t    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n772\t    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n773\t        return next.run(req).await;\n774\t    }\n775\t\n776\t    // Extract CSRF token from header\n777\t    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n778\t        Some(token) => token,\n779\t        None => {\n780\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n781\t                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n782\t                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n783\t            )\n784\t            .into_response();\n785\t        }\n786\t    };\n787\t\n788\t    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n789\t    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n790\t        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n791\t        None => {\n792\t            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n793\t            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n794\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n795\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n796\t                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n797\t            )\n798\t            .into_response();\n799\t        }\n800\t    };\n801\t\n802\t    // Validate CSRF token against session\n803\t    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n804\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n805\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n806\t            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n807\t        )\n808\t        .into_response();\n809\t    };\n810\t\n811\t    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n812\t        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n813\t        Ok(None) => {\n814\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n815\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n816\t                \"Admin session not found.\",\n817\t            )\n818\t            .into_response();\n819\t        }\n820\t        Err(e) => {\n821\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n822\t            return MeilisearchError::new(\n823\t                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n824\t                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n825\t            )\n826\t            .into_response();\n827\t        }\n828\t    };\n829\t\n830\t    // Check if revoked\n831\t    if session.revoked {\n832\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n833\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n834\t            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n835\t        )\n836\t        .into_response();\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    // Check expiration\n840\t    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n841\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n842\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n843\t        .as_secs() as i64;\n844\t    if session.expires_at < now {\n845\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n846\t            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n847\t            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n848\t        )\n849\t        .into_response();\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n853\t    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n854\t        return MeilisearchError::new(\n855\t            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n856\t            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n857\t        )\n858\t        .into_response();\n859\t    }\n860\t\n861\t    next.run(req).await\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n865\t// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n866\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n867\t\n868\t/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n869\t/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n870\t/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n871\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n872\tpub enum RateLimitBucket {\n873\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n874\t    AdminLogin(String),\n875\t    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n876\t    SearchUi(String),\n877\t}\n878\t\n879\t/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n880\t/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n881\t/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n882\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n883\tpub struct RateLimiter;\n884\t\n885\timpl RateLimiter {\n886\t    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n887\t        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n888\t    }\n889\t}\n890\t\n891\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n892\t// Helpers\n893\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n894\t\n895\tfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n896\t    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n897\t        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n898\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n899\t        .as_secs()\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n903\t// Tests\n904\t// = test_state();\n1170\t        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n1171\t            &Method::GET,\n1172\t            \"/_miroir/metrics\",\n1173\t            None,\n1174\t            &state,\n1175\t        );\n1176\t        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n1177\t    }\n1178\t\n1179\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1180\t    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n1181\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1182\t\n1183\t    #[test]\n1184\t    fn jwt_shape_probe_accepts_valid_shape() {\n1185\t };\n1274\t        let header = JwtHeader {\n1275\t            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n1276\t            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n1277\t            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n1278\t -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1465\t\n1466\t    #[test]\n1467\t    fn full_rotation_e2e() {\n1468\t        let secret_v1 = \"version-1-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1469\t        let secret_v2 = \"version-2-secret-key-32bytes-long!\";\n1470\t\n1471\t        // Pre-rotation: only v1\n1472\t        let pre = AuthState {\n1473\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1474\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1475\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1476\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1477\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1478\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1479\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1480\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1481\t            ).unwrap(),\n1482\t        };\n1483\t        let token_v1 = pre.sign_jwt(\"alice\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1484\t        assert!(pre.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1485\t\n1486\t        // During rotation: v2 primary, v1 previous\n1487\t        let during = AuthState {\n1488\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1489\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1490\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1491\t            jwt_previous: Some(secret_v1.into()),\n1492\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1493\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1494\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1495\t assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_ok());\n1500\t        // New tokens work too\n1501\t        let token_v2 = during.sign_jwt(\"bob\", \"idx\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n1502\t        assert!(during.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n1503\t\n1504\t        // Post-rotation: only v2\n1505\t        let post = AuthState {\n1506\t            master_key: \"m\".into(),\n1507\t            admin_key: \"a\".into(),\n1508\t            jwt_primary: Some(secret_v2.into()),\n1509\t            jwt_previous: None,\n1510\t            seal_key: test_key(),\n1511\t            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n1512\t            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n1513\t                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n1514\t            ).unwrap(),\n1515\t        };\n1516\t        // New token still works\n1517\t        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v2).is_ok());\n1518\t        // Old token is rejected (signed with v1, no previous loaded)\n1519\t        assert!(post.validate_jwt(&token_v1).is_err());\n1520\t    }\n1521\t\n1522\t    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n1523\t    // Rule 2 — #[test]\n1686\t    fn constant_time_eq_empty() {\n1687\t        assert!(constant_time_compare(b\"\", b\"\"));\n1688\t    }\n1689\t\n1690\t    /// Timing-injection harness: verify no measurable delta between\n1691\t    /// \"all bytes wrong\" and \"one byte wrong\" comparisons at the same length.\n1692\t    #[test]\n1693\t    fn constant_time_no_timing_leak() {\n1694\t        use std::time::Instant;\n1695\t\n1696\t        let expected = b\"admin-key-456\";\n1697\t        let all_wrong = b\"xxxxxxxxxxxxx\";\n1698\t        let one_wrong = b\"admin-key-457\";\n1699\t\n1700\t        let iterations = 100_000u64;\n1701\t\n1702\t        let start = Instant::now();\n1703\t        for _ in 0..iterations {\n1704\t            let _ = constant_time_compare(all_wrong, expected);\n1705\t        }\n1706\t        let all_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n1707\t\n1708\t        let start = Instant::now();\n1709\t        for _ in 0..iterations {\n1710\t            let _ = constant_time_compare(one_wrong, expected);\n1711\t        }\n1712\t        let one_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n1713\t\n1714\t        let ratio = all_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64() / one_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64();\n1715\t        assert!(\n1716\t            ratio > 0.5 && ratio < 2.0,\n1717\t            \"Timing ratio {} suggests non-constant-time comparison: all_wrong={:?}, one_wrong={:?}\",\n1718\t            ratio,\n1719\t            all_wrong_duration,\n1720\t            one_wrong_duration,\n1721\t        );\n1722\t    }\n1723\t\n1724\t    //    (Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/en-US\"),\n1799\t            (Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/fr\"),\n1800\t            (Method::POST, \"/_miroir/admin/login\"),\n1801\t            (Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/products/session\"),\n1802\t            (Method::GET, \"/_miroir/ui/search/users/session\"),\n1803\t            (Method::GET, \"/ui/search/products\"),\n1804\t            (Method::GET, \"/ui/search/users\"),\n1805\t        ];\n1806\t        for (method, path) in cases {\n1807\t            assert!(\n1808\t                is_dispatch_exempt(&method, path),\n1809\t                \"Expected ({}, {}) to be dispatch-exempt\",\n1810\t                method,\n1811\t                path,\n1812\t            );\n1813\t        }\n1814\t
