Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5 rules 0–5.\n//!\n//! Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously:\n//! the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them\n//! deterministically in the order specified by §5.\n//!\n//! JWT signing-secret rotation (plan §9):\n//! - Primary secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`) signs new tokens; `kid` header identifies it.\n//! - Optional previous secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`) is present only during\n//!   the rotation overlap window. Validation accepts either secret.\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{FromRef, Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, Method},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::{IntoResponse, Response},\n};\nuse base64::{engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD, Engine as _};\nuse dashmap::DashMap;\nuse hmac::{Hmac, Mac};\nuse miroir_core::{task_store::TaskStore, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse prometheus::Counter;\nuse rand::RngCore;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse sha2::Sha256;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse subtle::ConstantTimeEq;\n\nuse crate::admin_session::{self, SealKey};\n\ntype HmacSha256 = Hmac<Sha256>;\n\n/// Extension carried in the request after successful cookie unseal.\n/// Handlers extract this to look up the session in the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct AdminSessionId(pub String);\n\n/// State for CSRF middleware, combining AuthState with task store access.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct CsrfState {\n    pub auth: AuthState,\n    pub redis_store: Option<miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore>,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// JWT claims (plan §13.21)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Claims embedded in a search UI JWT session token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JwtClaims {\n    /// Subject — user identifier or \"anonymous\".\n    pub sub: String,\n    /// Index this token grants access to.\n    pub idx: String,\n    /// Granted scope (e.g. \"search\").\n    pub scope: String,\n    /// Issued-at timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n    pub iat: u64,\n    /// Expiration timestamp (seconds since epoch).\n    pub exp: u64,\n}\n\n/// Key ID embedded in the JWT header to identify which secret signed it.\nconst KID_PRIMARY: &str = \"primary\";\nconst KID_PREVIOUS: &str = \"previous\";\n\n/// JWT header (always HS256).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct JwtHeader {\n    alg: String,\n    kid: String,\n    typ: String,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Minimal HS256 JWT encode / decode (no external JWT crate needed)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Encode and sign a JWT with the given secret.\nfn jwt_encode(header: &JwtHeader, claims: &JwtClaims, secret: &[u8]) -> Result<String, String> {\n    let header_json = serde_json::to_string(header).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n    let claims_json = serde_json::to_string(claims).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;\n\n    let header_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(header_json.as_bytes());\n    let payload_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(claims_json.as_bytes());\n\n    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64);\n\n    let mut mac =\n        HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).map_err(|e| format!(\"HMAC init: {}\", e))?;\n    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n    let sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n    let sig_b64 = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(sig);\n\n    Ok(format!(\"{}.{}.{}\", header_b64, payload_b64, sig_b64))\n}\n\n/// Decode and verify a JWT with the given secret. Returns (header, claims).\nfn jwt_decode(\n    token: &str,\n    secret: &[u8],\n) -> Result<(JwtHeader, JwtClaims), JwtValidationError> {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 3 {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n    }\n\n    let header_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[0])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n    let header: JwtHeader =\n        serde_json::from_slice(&header_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n\n    if header.alg != \"HS256\" {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Malformed);\n    }\n\n    // Verify signature\n    let signing_input = format!(\"{}.{}\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n    let mut mac = HmacSha256::new_from_slice(secret).unwrap();\n    mac.update(signing_input.as_bytes());\n    let expected_sig = mac.finalize().into_bytes();\n\n    let actual_sig = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[2])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)?;\n\n    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n    let sig_valid: bool = actual_sig.ct_eq(&expected_sig).into();\n    if !sig_valid {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature);\n    }\n\n    // Decode claims\n    let claims_bytes = URL_SAFE_NO_PAD\n        .decode(parts[1])\n        .map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n    let claims: JwtClaims =\n        serde_json::from_slice(&claims_bytes).map_err(|_| JwtValidationError::Malformed)?;\n\n    // Check expiration with 30s leeway\n    let now = epoch_seconds();\n    if claims.exp + 30 < now {\n        return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired);\n    }\n\n    Ok((header, claims))\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Auth state (shared via axum State)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Configuration needed by the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthState {\n    pub master_key: String,\n    pub admin_key: String,\n    /// HMAC secret for signing/validating search UI JWTs (primary).\n    pub jwt_primary: Option<String>,\n    /// Optional previous secret active during rotation overlap window.\n    pub jwt_previous: Option<String>,\n    /// Key for sealing/unsealing admin session cookies (XChaCha20-Poly1305).\n    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n    /// In-memory set of revoked admin session IDs (populated on logout, Pub/Sub).\n    pub revoked_sessions: Arc<DashMap<String, ()>>,\n    /// Counter for revoked admin sessions (miroir_admin_session_revoked_total).\n    pub admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for AuthState {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"AuthState\")\n            .field(\"master_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .field(\"jwt_primary\", &self.jwt_primary.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n            .field(\"jwt_previous\", &self.jwt_previous.as_ref().map(|_| \"[set]\"))\n            .field(\"seal_key\", &self.seal_key)\n            .field(\"revoked_sessions\", &self.revoked_sessions.len())\n            .finish_non_exhaustive()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// JWT signing / validation helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nimpl AuthState {\n    /// Create a new signed JWT session token for the given index.\n    /// Always signs with the primary secret; `kid` header identifies it.\n    pub fn sign_jwt(&self, sub: &str, idx: &str, scope: &str, ttl_s: u64) -> Option<String> {\n        let secret = self.jwt_primary.as_ref()?;\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: sub.to_string(),\n            idx: idx.to_string(),\n            scope: scope.to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + ttl_s,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        jwt_encode(&header, &claims, secret.as_bytes()).ok()\n    }\n\n    /// Validate a JWT string against either the primary or previous secret.\n    /// Returns the parsed claims if validation succeeds, or an error.\n    pub fn validate_jwt(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, JwtValidationError> {\n        // Try primary secret first\n        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_primary {\n            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n                _ => {} // signature mismatch — try previous\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Try previous secret (rotation overlap window)\n        if let Some(ref secret) = self.jwt_previous {\n            if secret.is_empty() {\n                return Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty);\n            }\n            match jwt_decode(token, secret.as_bytes()) {\n                Ok((_header, claims)) => return Ok(claims),\n                Err(JwtValidationError::Expired) => return Err(JwtValidationError::Expired),\n                _ => {} // signature mismatch\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Errors returned by JWT validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum JwtValidationError {\n    /// Token structure is invalid or alg is not HS256.\n    Malformed,\n    /// HMAC signature did not match any loaded secret.\n    InvalidSignature,\n    /// Token has expired.\n    Expired,\n    /// `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` is set to the empty string (leak response).\n    PreviousSecretEmpty,\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSRF token generation (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Generate a cryptographically random CSRF token.\n/// Returns a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte token.\npub fn generate_csrf_token() -> String {\n    let mut bytes = [0u8; 32];\n    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n    URL_SAFE_NO_PAD.encode(&bytes)\n}\n\n/// Extract the CSRF token from the `X-CSRF-Token` header.\npub fn extract_csrf_token(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n    headers.get(\"X-CSRF-Token\")?.to_str().ok().map(String::from)\n}\n\n/// Constant-time comparison of CSRF tokens.\npub fn constant_time_csrf_compare(token: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {\n    constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), expected.as_bytes())\n}\n\n/// Validate a CSRF token against the expected session token.\n/// Returns Ok(()) if the token matches, or a CsrfMismatch error.\npub fn validate_csrf_token(provided: &str, expected: &str) -> Result<(), MiroirCode> {\n    if constant_time_csrf_compare(provided, expected) {\n        Ok(())\n    } else {\n        Err(MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Origin validation (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Result of origin validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum OriginVerdict {\n    /// Origin is allowed.\n    Allowed,\n    /// Origin is not in the allowed list.\n    Forbidden,\n    /// No Origin/Referer header present (for same-origin requests).\n    Missing,\n}\n\n/// Validate the Origin header against the allowed origins list.\n/// Handles special \"same-origin\" value by comparing against the Host header.\npub fn validate_origin(\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    allowed_origins: &[String],\n    is_same_origin_by_default: bool,\n) -> OriginVerdict {\n    // Try Origin header first (preferred for POST/DELETE/PUT)\n    let origin = headers.get(\"origin\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok());\n\n    // Fall back to Referer header (for navigational requests)\n    let referer = origin.or_else(|| headers.get(\"referer\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()));\n\n    let provided_origin = match referer {\n        Some(o) => o,\n        None => {\n            // No Origin or Referer header - for same-origin requests, this is acceptable\n            return if is_same_origin_by_default {\n                OriginVerdict::Missing\n            } else {\n                OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n            };\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Strip path from Referer to get origin\n    let provided_origin = if let Some(ref_hdr) = headers.get(\"referer\") {\n        if let Ok(ref_val) = ref_hdr.to_str() {\n            // Find the first '/' after \"https://\" (skip the first 8 chars: \"https://\")\n            if let Some(idx) = ref_val.chars().enumerate().skip(8).find(|(_, c)| *c == '/').map(|(i, _)| i) {\n                &ref_val[..idx]\n            } else {\n                ref_val\n            }\n        } else {\n            provided_origin\n        }\n    } else {\n        provided_origin\n    };\n\n    // Check against allowed origins\n    for allowed in allowed_origins {\n        // Special \"same-origin\" value - compare against Host header\n        if allowed == \"same-origin\" {\n            if let Some(host) = headers.get(\"host\").and_then(|h| h.to_str().ok()) {\n                // Construct origin from scheme (https) and host\n                let same_origin = format!(\"https://{}\", host);\n                if provided_origin == same_origin || provided_origin == host {\n                    return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n                }\n            }\n        } else if allowed == \"*\" {\n            // Wildcard allows any origin\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        } else if provided_origin == allowed {\n            return OriginVerdict::Allowed;\n        }\n    }\n\n    OriginVerdict::Forbidden\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSP header builder (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Build a CSP header value by merging base template with overrides.\n/// Overrides are merged additively - they never replace the base template.\npub fn build_csp_header(\n    base_template: &str,\n    overrides: &miroir_core::config::CspOverridesConfig,\n) -> String {\n    let mut directives: Vec<(String, Vec<String>)> = base_template\n        .split(';')\n        .map(|s| s.trim())\n        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())\n        .map(|directive| {\n            let parts: Vec<&str> = directive.splitn(2, ' ').collect();\n            if parts.len() == 2 {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![parts[1].to_string()])\n            } else {\n                (parts[0].to_lowercase(), vec![])\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Helper to merge overrides into a directive\n    let merge_into = |directives: &mut Vec<(String, Vec<String>)>,\n                      name: &str,\n                      values: &[String]| {\n        if values.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n        let name_lower = name.to_lowercase();\n        if let Some(entry) = directives.iter_mut().find(|(n, _)| n == &name_lower) {\n            // Append to existing directive\n            entry.1.extend(values.iter().cloned());\n            entry.1.dedup(); // Remove duplicates\n        } else {\n            // Add new directive\n            directives.push((name_lower, values.to_vec()));\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Merge each override category\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"script-src\", &overrides.script_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"img-src\", &overrides.img_src);\n    merge_into(&mut directives, \"connect-src\", &overrides.connect_src);\n\n    // Rebuild CSP string\n    directives\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(name, values)| {\n            if values.is_empty() {\n                name\n            } else {\n                format!(\"{} {}\", name, values.join(\" \"))\n            }\n        })\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        .join(\"; \")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Dispatch verdict\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Result of the bearer-token dispatch chain.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthVerdict {\n    /// Request is dispatch-exempt (rule 0); handler decides auth.\n    Exempt,\n    /// Authenticated with the given token kind.\n    Authenticated(TokenKind),\n    /// Bearer token looked like a JWT but failed validation (rule 1).\n    JwtInvalid,\n    /// JWT was signature-valid but scope was insufficient (rule 1).\n    JwtScopeDenied,\n    /// No matching key / missing Authorization (rule 4).\n    InvalidAuth,\n}\n\n/// Which key or token type satisfied authentication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    MasterKey,\n    AdminKey,\n    /// JWT validated against primary or previous secret.\n    Jwt,\n    /// Admin session cookie — sealed session ID validated against task store.\n    AdminSession,\n}\n\nimpl AuthVerdict {\n    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AuthVerdict::Exempt | AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_))\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt check\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true when `(method, path)` is in the exhaustive dispatch-exempt list\n/// (plan §5 rule 5). Exempt endpoints run their handler directly; rules 1–4\n/// are never consulted.\npub fn is_dispatch_exempt(method: &Method, path: &str) -> bool {\n    // `GET /health` — unauthenticated liveness probe (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/health\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /version` — unauthenticated version endpoint (Meilisearch-compatible)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/version\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ready` — unauthenticated readiness probe (plan §10)\n    if method == Method::GET && path == \"/_miroir/ready\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated public locale fetch\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/locale/\") {\n            // Must have at least one path segment after the prefix\n            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains(\"//\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body\n    if method == Method::POST && path == \"/_miroir/admin/login\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per search_ui.auth.mode\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/_miroir/ui/search/\") {\n            let segments: Vec<&str> = rest.split('/').collect();\n            if segments.len() == 2 && segments[1] == \"session\" && !segments[0].is_empty() {\n                return true;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA entry point\n    if method == Method::GET {\n        if let Some(rest) = path.strip_prefix(\"/ui/search/\") {\n            // Single non-empty segment (the index name)\n            return !rest.is_empty() && !rest.contains('/');\n        }\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true if `token` has the structural shape of a JWT (three\n/// dot-separated base64url segments).\npub fn probe_jwt_shape(token: &str) -> bool {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 3 {\n        return false;\n    }\n    // Each segment should be non-empty and look like base64url\n    parts.iter().all(|s| {\n        !s.is_empty()\n            && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '-' || c == '_' || c == '=')\n    })\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper — admin path check\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Returns true if `path` starts with `/_miroir/` (admin surface).\npub fn is_admin_path(path: &str) -> bool {\n    path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\")\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Constant-time opaque key comparison\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Constant-time comparison of an opaque token against an expected key.\n/// Prevents timing side-channels on secret key values.\npub fn constant_time_compare(token: &[u8], expected: &[u8]) -> bool {\n    token.ct_eq(expected).into()\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Core dispatch — rules 0–4\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Execute the full bearer-token dispatch chain for a request.\n///\n/// `bearer_token` is the raw value after stripping `\"Bearer \"` from the\n/// `Authorization` header (may be `None` if the header is absent).\npub fn dispatch_bearer(\n    method: &Method,\n    path: &str,\n    bearer_token: Option<&str>,\n    state: &AuthState,\n) -> AuthVerdict {\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt endpoints skip all auth checks\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(method, path) {\n        return AuthVerdict::Exempt;\n    }\n\n    let token = match bearer_token {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth, // Rule 4 — missing auth\n    };\n\n    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe, then full validation\n    if probe_jwt_shape(token) {\n        match state.validate_jwt(token) {\n            Ok(_claims) => return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt),\n            Err(JwtValidationError::PreviousSecretEmpty) => {\n                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n            }\n            Err(_) => {\n                return AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Rule 2 — admin-path opaque-token match\n    if is_admin_path(path) {\n        if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n            return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::AdminKey);\n        }\n        return AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth; // Rule 4\n    }\n\n    // Rule 3 — master-key match (non-admin paths)\n    if constant_time_compare(token.as_bytes(), state.master_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::MasterKey);\n    }\n\n    // Rule 4 — mismatch\n    AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// X-Admin-Key short-circuit\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Check the `X-Admin-Key` header for admin endpoints.\n/// Returns `true` if the header is present and matches `admin_key`.\n/// Evaluated independently of the bearer chain — short-circuits directly.\npub fn check_x_admin_key(headers: &HeaderMap, admin_key: &[u8]) -> bool {\n    match headers.get(\"X-Admin-Key\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()) {\n        Some(key) => constant_time_compare(key.as_bytes(), admin_key),\n        None => false,\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Axum middleware\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Extract the bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <value>`.\nfn extract_bearer(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<&str> {\n    let auth = headers.get(\"authorization\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n    auth.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \")\n}\n\n/// Extract the sealed admin session cookie value from the Cookie header.\npub fn extract_admin_session_cookie(headers: &HeaderMap) -> Option<String> {\n    let cookie_header = headers.get(\"cookie\")?.to_str().ok()?;\n    for pair in cookie_header.split(';') {\n        let pair = pair.trim();\n        if let Some(value) = pair.strip_prefix(&format!(\"{}=\", admin_session::COOKIE_NAME)) {\n            return Some(value.to_string());\n        }\n    }\n    None\n}\n\n/// Unseal an admin session cookie, returning the session ID.\npub fn unseal_admin_cookie(cookie_value: &str, key: &SealKey) -> Result<String, admin_session::SealError> {\n    admin_session::unseal_session(cookie_value, key)\n}\n\n/// Axum middleware implementing the bearer-token dispatch chain (plan §5).\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<AuthState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt: skip everything, handler decides auth\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // X-Admin-Key short-circuit for admin endpoints\n    if is_admin_path(&path) && check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Admin session cookie check for admin endpoints (plan §9, §13.19).\n    // If a sealed admin session cookie is present, unseal it and authenticate.\n    // Revoked sessions are rejected immediately.\n    if is_admin_path(&path) {\n        if let Some(cookie_value) = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()) {\n            match unseal_admin_cookie(&cookie_value, &state.seal_key) {\n                Ok(session_id) => {\n                    // Check revocation cache (populated on logout + Pub/Sub).\n                    if state.revoked_sessions.contains_key(&session_id) {\n                        return MeilisearchError::new(\n                            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n                        )\n                        .into_response();\n                    }\n                    let mut req = req;\n                    req.extensions_mut().insert(AdminSessionId(session_id));\n                    return next.run(req).await;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    // Cookie tampering or wrong seal key (e.g. cross-pod key\n                    // mismatch in HA).  Log a warning so operators can diagnose\n                    // ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY divergence across pods.\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        path = %path,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"admin session cookie unseal failed — tampered cookie or cross-pod key mismatch\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Extract bearer token\n    let bearer = extract_bearer(req.headers());\n\n    // Run the dispatch chain\n    let verdict = dispatch_bearer(&method, &path, bearer, &state);\n\n    match verdict {\n        AuthVerdict::Authenticated(_) | AuthVerdict::Exempt => next.run(req).await,\n        AuthVerdict::JwtInvalid => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n            \"The provided JWT is invalid or expired.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n        AuthVerdict::JwtScopeDenied => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n            \"The provided JWT does not grant access to this resource.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n        AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth => MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"The provided authorization is invalid.\",\n        )\n        .into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSRF validation middleware (plan §9)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// CSRF middleware that validates `X-CSRF-Token` on state-changing requests.\n///\n/// Bypasses CSRF check when:\n/// - Request is authenticated via Bearer token (not admin session cookie)\n/// - X-Admin-Key header is present\n/// - Request method is safe (GET, HEAD, OPTIONS)\n/// - Path is dispatch-exempt\n///\n/// For admin session cookie auth, requires `X-CSRF-Token` header to match\n/// the token stored in the session.\npub async fn csrf_middleware(\n    State(state): State<CsrfState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip CSRF for safe methods\n    if matches!(method, Method::GET | Method::HEAD | Method::OPTIONS) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF for non-admin paths\n    if !is_admin_path(&path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF for dispatch-exempt endpoints\n    if is_dispatch_exempt(&method, &path) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Skip CSRF if X-Admin-Key is present (bypasses CSRF)\n    if check_x_admin_key(req.headers(), state.auth.admin_key.as_bytes()) {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Check if authenticated via admin session cookie\n    let has_session_cookie = extract_admin_session_cookie(req.headers()).is_some();\n    let has_bearer_token = extract_bearer(req.headers()).is_some();\n\n    // CSRF only applies to session-cookie auth, not bearer tokens\n    if !has_session_cookie || has_bearer_token {\n        return next.run(req).await;\n    }\n\n    // Extract CSRF token from header\n    let csrf_token = match extract_csrf_token(req.headers()) {\n        Some(token) => token,\n        None => {\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::MissingCsrf,\n                \"CSRF token is required for state-changing requests.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Get session ID from extensions (set by auth_middleware)\n    let session_id = match req.extensions().get::<AdminSessionId>() {\n        Some(id) => id.0.clone(),\n        None => {\n            // Session cookie was present but auth_middleware didn't set AdminSessionId\n            // This means the session was invalid/expired/revoked\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Admin session is invalid or expired.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Validate CSRF token against session\n    let Some(redis_store) = state.redis_store.as_ref() else {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin sessions require Redis task store.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    };\n\n    let session = match redis_store.get_admin_session(&session_id) {\n        Ok(Some(s)) => s,\n        Ok(None) => {\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Admin session not found.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, session_prefix = &session_id[..session_id.len().min(8)], \"failed to get admin session for CSRF validation\");\n            return MeilisearchError::new(\n                MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n                \"Failed to validate session.\",\n            )\n            .into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Check if revoked\n    if session.revoked {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin session has been revoked.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    // Check expiration\n    let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs() as i64;\n    if session.expires_at < now {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n            \"Admin session has expired.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    // Constant-time compare CSRF tokens\n    if !constant_time_csrf_compare(&csrf_token, &session.csrf_token) {\n        return MeilisearchError::new(\n            MiroirCode::CsrfMismatch,\n            \"CSRF token does not match the session token.\",\n        )\n        .into_response();\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rate-limit hook types (Phase 2 in-memory stub, Phase 6 multi-pod)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Rate-limit bucket key types wired into the dispatch chain.\n/// Phase 2 keeps these as in-memory counters; Phase 6 will back them\n/// with the task store (Redis/SQLite).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum RateLimitBucket {\n    /// `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>`\n    AdminLogin(String),\n    /// `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>`\n    SearchUi(String),\n}\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter (Phase 2 stub). Always returns `Ok(())` — actual\n/// enforcement is deferred to Phase 6 multi-pod. The hook is wired here so\n/// handlers can call `limiter.check()` without cfg-gating.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct RateLimiter;\n\nimpl RateLimiter {\n    pub fn check(&self, _bucket: &RateLimitBucket) -> Result<(), ()> {\n        Ok(()) // Phase 2: always allow\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn epoch_seconds() -> u64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn test_key() -> SealKey {\n        SealKey::from_bytes([42u8; 32])\n    }\n\n    fn test_state() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: None,\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_state_with_jwt() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(\"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\".to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: None,\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn test_state_with_dual_jwt() -> AuthState {\n        AuthState {\n            master_key: \"master-key-123\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"admin-key-456\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n            admin_session_revoked_total: Counter::with_opts(\n                prometheus::Opts::new(\"test_revoked_total\", \"test\")\n            ).unwrap(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 0 — dispatch-exempt tests\n    //    fn metrics_rejects_missing_auth() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/_miroir/metrics\",\n            None,\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::InvalidAuth);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Rule 1 — JWT-shape probe\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn jwt_shape_probe_accepts_valid_shape() {\n        assert!(probe_jwt_shape(\"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.abc123\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn jwt_shape_probe_rejects_two_parts() {\n jwt_shape_probe_rejects_empty() {\n        assert!(!probe_jwt_shape(\"\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn jwt_shape_probe_rejects_opaque_token() {\n        assert!(!probe_jwt_shape(\"admin-key-456\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn jwt_on_non_admin_path_with_no_secret_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n        let state = test_state(); // no JWT secrets configured\n        let jwt = \"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.abc123\";\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT signing and validation — primary secret\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn sign_and_validate_primary_jwt() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let claims = state.validate_jwt(&token).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claims.sub, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.idx, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(claims.scope, \"search\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn signed_jwt_authenticates_via_dispatch() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n\n        let verdict = dispatch_bearer(\n            &Method::GET,\n            \"/indexes/products\",\n            Some(&token),\n            &state,\n        );\n        assert_eq!(verdict, AuthVerdict::Authenticated(TokenKind::Jwt));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn expired_jwt_returns_jwt_invalid() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now - 3600,\n            exp: now - 100, // expired well beyond 30s leeway\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PRIMARY.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let token = jwt_encode(\n            &header,\n            &claims,\n            state.jwt_primary.as_ref().unwrap().as_bytes(),\n        )\n        .unwrap();\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::Expired));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn tampered_signature_returns_invalid_signature() {\n        let state = test_state_with_jwt();\n        let mut token = state.sign_jwt(\"user1\", \"products\", \"search\", 900).unwrap();\n        // Tamper with the signature\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = token.split('.').collect();\n        token = format!(\"{}.{}.tampered_sig\", parts[0], parts[1]);\n\n        let result = state.validate_jwt(&token);\n        assert_eq!(result, Err(JwtValidationError::InvalidSignature));\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // JWT dual-secret rotation validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rotation_old_token_validates_via_previous_secret() {\n        let primary = \"test-secret-primary-key-32byte\";\n        let previous = \"test-secret-previous-key-32byte\";\n\n        // Sign token with the previous secret\n        let now = epoch_seconds();\n        let claims = JwtClaims {\n            sub: \"user1\".to_string(),\n            idx: \"products\".to_string(),\n            scope: \"search\".to_string(),\n            iat: now,\n            exp: now + 900,\n        };\n        let header = JwtHeader {\n            alg: \"HS256\".to_string(),\n            kid: KID_PREVIOUS.to_string(),\n            typ: \"JWT\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let old_token = jwt_encode(&header, &claims, previous.as_bytes()).unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate rotation — new primary, old primary as previous\n        let state = AuthState {\n            master_key: \"m\".to_string(),\n            admin_key: \"a\".to_string(),\n            jwt_primary: Some(primary.to_string()),\n            jwt_previous: Some(previous.to_string()),\n            seal_key: test_key(),\n            revoked_sessions: Arc::new(DashMap::new()),\n       let expected = b\"admin-key-456\";\n        let all_wrong = b\"xxxxxxxxxxxxx\";\n        let one_wrong = b\"admin-key-457\";\n\n        let iterations = 100_000u64;\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(all_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let all_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        for _ in 0..iterations {\n            let _ = constant_time_compare(one_wrong, expected);\n        }\n        let one_wrong_duration = start.elapsed();\n\n        let ratio = all_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64() / one_wrong_duration.as_secs_f64();\n        assert!(\n            ratio > 0.5 && ratio < 2.0,\n            \"Timing ratio {} suggests non-constant-time comparison: all_wrong={:?}, one_wrong={:?}\",\n            ratio,\n            all_wrong_duration,\n            one_wrong_duration,\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n2\t\n3\tuse std::time::Instant;\n4\t\n5\tuse axum::{\n6\t    extract::{Request, State},\n7\t    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, extensions.\n25\t///\n26\t/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n27\t/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n28\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n29\tpub struct RequestId(pub String);\n30\t\n31\timpl RequestId {\n32\t    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n33\t    ///\n34\t    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n35\t    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n36\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n37\t        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n38\t        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n39\t        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n40\t        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n41\t        hasher.write(bytes);\n42\t        let hash = hasher.finish();\n43\t        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n44\t        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n45\t    }\n46\t\n47\t    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n48\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n49\t        &self.0\n50\t    }\n51\t\n52\t    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n53\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n54\t        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n55\t            Some(Self(s))\n56\t        } else {\n57\t            None\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\t/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n63\t///\n64\t/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n65\t/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n66\t#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\n67\tpub struct SessionId(pub String);\n68\t\n69\timpl Default for SessionId {\n70\t    fn default() -> Self {\n71\t        Self(String::new())\n72\t    }\n73\t}\n74\t\n75\timpl SessionId {\n76\t    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n77\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n78\t        &self.0\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n82\t    ///\n83\t    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n84\t    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n85\t        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n86\t            Some(Self(s))\n87\t        } else {\n88\t            None\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t}\n92\t\n93\tpub async fn request_id_middleware(\n94\t    mut req: Request,\n95\t    next: Next,\n96\t) -> Response {\n97\t    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n98\t    let request_id = req\n99\t        .headers()\n100\t        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n101\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n102\t        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n103\t        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n104\t\n105\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n106\t    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n107\t\n108\t    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n109\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n110\t        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    // Process the request\n114\t    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n115\t\n116\t    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n117\t    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n118\t        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t    response\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n125\t///\n126\t/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n127\t/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n128\tpub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n129\t    mut req: Request,\n130\t    next: Next,\n131\t) -> Response {\n132\t    // Extract session ID from header if present\n133\t    let session_id = req\n134\t        .headers()\n135\t        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n136\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n137\t        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n138\t\n139\t    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n140\t    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n141\t        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n142\t    }\n143\t\n144\t    next.run(req).await\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t\n148\t/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n149\t#[derive(Clone)]\n150\tpub struct TelemetryState {\n151\t    pub metrics: Metrics,\n152\t    pub pod_id: String,\n153\t}\n154\t\n155\timpl TelemetryState {\n156\t    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n157\t        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n158\t        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n159\t    }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\n163\tpub struct Metrics {\n164\t    registry: Registry,\n165\t\n166\t    // ── Request Option<Counter>,\n200\t    multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n201\t\n202\t    // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics (feature-gated) ──\n203\t metrics (feature-gated) ──\n208\t    cdc_events_published_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n209\t    cdc_lag_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n210\t    cdc_buffer_bytes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n211\t    cdc_dropped_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n212\t    cdc_events_suppressed_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n213\t\n214\t    // ── §13.14 TTL metrics (feature-gated) ──\n215\t    ttl_documents_expired_total: shadow_errors_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n229\t\n230\t    // ── §13.17 ILM metrics (feature-gated) ──\n231\t    rollover_events_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n232\t    rollover_active_indexes: Option<GaugeVec>,\n233\t    rollover_documents_expired_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n234\t    rollover_last_action_seconds: Option<GaugeVec>,\n235\t\n236\t    // ── §13.18 Canary metrics (feature-gated) Option<CounterVec>,\n245\t\n246\t    // ── §13.20 Explain metrics (feature-gated) ──\n247\t    explain_requests_total: Option<Counter>,\n248\t    explain_warnings_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n249\t    explain_execute_total: Option<Counter>,\n250\t\n251\t    // ── §13.21 Search UI metrics (feature-gated) ──\n252\t    search_ui_sessions_total: Option<Counter>,\n253\t    search_ui_queries_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n254\t    search_ui_zero_hits_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n255\t    search_ui_click_through_total: Option<CounterVec>,\n256\t    search_ui_p95_ms: Option<GaugeVec>,\n257\t\n258\t    // ── §14.9 Resource-pressure metrics (always present) ──\n259\t    memory_pressure: Gauge,\n260\t    cpu_throttled_seconds_total: Counter,\n261\t    request_queue_depth: Gauge,\n262\t    background_queue_depth: GaugeVec,\n263\t    peer_pod_count: Gauge,\n264\t    leader: GaugeVec,\n265\t    owned_shards_count: Gauge,\n266\t\n267\t    // ── Admin session sealing metrics (always present) ──\n268\t    admin_session_key_generated: Gauge,\n269\t    admin_session_revoked_total: Counter,\n270\t\n271\t    // ── §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast metrics (always present) ──\n272\t    settings_broadcast_phase: GaugeVec,\n273\t    settings_hash_mismatch_total: Counter,\n274\t    settings_drift_repair_total: CounterVec,\n275\t    settings_version: GaugeVec,\n276\t    settings_divergence_alert_total: Counter,\n277\t    frozen_indexes: GaugeVec,\n278\t\n279\t    // ── §13.7 Alias metrics (always present) ──\n280\t    alias_resolutions_total: CounterVec,\n281\t    alias_flips_total: CounterVec,\n282\t\n283\t    // ── §13.6 Session pinning metrics (always present) ──\n284\t    session_active_count: Gauge,\n285\t    session_pin_enforced_total: CounterVec,\n286\t    session_wait_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n287\t    session_wait_timeout_total: CounterVec,\n288\t\n289\t    // ── §13.8 Anti-entropy metrics (always present) ──\n290\t    antientropy_shards_scanned_total: Counter,\n291\t    antientropy_mismatches_found_total: Counter,\n292\t    antientropy_docs_repaired_total: Counter,\n293\t    antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds: Gauge,\n294\t\n295\t    // ── §13.3 Adaptive replica selection metrics (always present) ──\n296\t    replica_selection_score: GaugeVec,\n297\t    replica_selection_exploration_total: Counter,\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\timpl Clone for Metrics {\n301\t    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n302\t        Self {\n303\t            registry: self.registry.clone(),\n304\t            request_duration: {\n395\t        Self::new(&MiroirConfig::default())\n396\t    }\n397\t}\n398\t\n399\timpl Metrics {\n400\t    pub fn new(config: &MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n401\t        let registry = Registry::new();\n402\t\n403\t        // ── Request metrics ──\n404\t        let request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n405\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_request_duration_seconds\", \"Request latency in seconds\")\n406\t                .buckets(vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0]),\n407\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n408\t        )\n409\t        .expect(\"failed to create request_duration histogram\");\n410\t\n411\t        let requests_total = CounterVec::new(\n412\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_total\", \"Total number of requests\"),\n413\t            &[\"method\", \"path_template\", \"status\"],\n414\t        )\n415\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_total counter\");\n416\t\n417\t        let requests_in_flight = Gauge::with_opts(\n418\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_requests_in_flight\", \"Number of requests currently being processed\"),\n419\t        )\n420\t        .expect(\"failed to create requests_in_flight gauge\");\n421\t\n422\t        // ── Node health metrics ──\n423\t        let node_healthy = GaugeVec::new(\n424\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_healthy\", \"Health status of backend nodes (1=healthy, 0=unhealthy)\"),\n425\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n426\t        )\n427\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_healthy gauge\");\n428\t\n429\t        let node_request_duration = HistogramVec::new(\n430\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_node_request_duration_seconds\", \"Latency of individual node requests\")\n431\t                .buckets(vec![0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0]),\n432\t            &[\"node_id\", \"operation\"],\n433\t        )\n434\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_request_duration histogram\");\n435\t\n436\t        let node_errors = CounterVec::new(\n437\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_node_errors_total\", \"Number of errors from backend nodes\"),\n438\t            &[\"node_id\", \"error_type\"],\n439\t        )\n440\t        .expect(\"failed to create node_errors counter\");\n441\t\n442\t        // ── Shard metrics ──\n443\t        let shard_coverage = Gauge::with_opts(\n444\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_coverage\", \"Fraction of shards with at least one healthy replica\"),\n445\t        )\n446\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_coverage gauge\");\n447\t\n448\t        let degraded_shards = Gauge::with_opts(\n449\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_degraded_shards_total\", \"Number of shards with reduced replica availability\"),\n450\t        )\n451\t        .expect(\"failed to create degraded_shards gauge\");\n452\t\n453\t        let shard_distribution = GaugeVec::new(\n454\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_shard_distribution\", \"Number of shards assigned to each node\"),\n455\t            &[\"node_id\"],\n456\t        )\n457\t        .expect(\"failed to create shard_distribution gauge\");\n458\t\n459\t        // ── Task metrics ──\n460\t        let task_processing_age = Histogram::with_opts(\n461\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_task_processing_age_seconds\", \"Time between task creation and processing start\")\n462\t                .buckets(vec![0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0]),\n463\t        )\n464\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_processing_age histogram\");\n465\t\n466\t        let tasks_total = CounterVec::new(\n467\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_tasks_total\", \"Total number of tasks by status\"),\n468\t            &[\"status\"],\n469\t        )\n470\t        .expect(\"failed to create tasks_total counter\");\n471\t\n472\t        let task_registry_size = Gauge::with_opts(\n473\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_task_registry_size\", \"Current number of tasks in the registry\"),\n474\t        )\n475\t        .expect(\"failed to create task_registry_size gauge\");\n476\t\n477\t        // ── Scatter-gather metrics ──\n478\t        let scatter_fan_out_size = Histogram::with_opts(\n479\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_scatter_fan_out_size\", \"Number of nodes in scatter operations\")\n480\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 5.0, 10.0, 20.0, 50.0]),\n481\t        )\n482\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_fan_out_size histogram\");\n483\t\n484\t        let scatter_partial_responses = Counter::with_opts(\n485\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total\", \"Number of scatter responses that were partial (some nodes failed)\"),\n486\t        )\n487\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_partial_responses counter\");\n488\t\n489\t        let scatter_retries = Counter::with_opts(\n490\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_scatter_retries_total\", \"Number of scatter retry attempts due to node failures\"),\n491\t        )\n492\t        .expect(\"failed to create scatter_retries counter\");\n493\t\n494\t        // ── Rebalancer metrics ──\n495\t        let rebalance_in_progress = Gauge::with_opts(\n496\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_in_progress\", \"Whether a rebalance is currently running (1=yes, 0=no)\"),\n497\t        )\n498\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_in_progress gauge\");\n499\t\n500\t        let rebalance_documents_migrated = Counter::with_opts(\n501\t            Opts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total\", \"Total number of documents migrated during rebalance\"),\n502\t        )\n503\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_documents_migrated counter\");\n504\t\n505\t        let rebalance_duration = Histogram::with_opts(\n506\t            HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds\", \"Duration of rebalance operations\")\n507\t                .buckets(vec![1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 30.0, 60.0, 300.0, 600.0, 1800.0, 3600.0]),\n508\t        )\n509\t        .expect(\"failed to create rebalance_duration histogram\");\n510\t\n511\t        // Register all metrics\n512\t        macro_rules! reg {\n513\t            ($m:expr) => {\n514\t                registry.register(Box::new($m.clone())).expect(concat!(\"failed to register \", stringify!($m)));\n515\t            };\n516\t        }\n517\t\n518\t        reg!(request_duration);\n519\t        reg!(requests_total);\n520\t        reg!(requests_in_flight);\n521\t        reg!(node_healthy);\n522\t        reg!(node_request_duration);\n523\t        reg!(node_errors);\n524\t        reg!(shard_coverage);\n525\t        reg!(degraded_shards);\n526\t        reg!(shard_distribution);\n527\t        reg!(task_processing_age);\n528\t        reg!(tasks_total);\n529\t        reg!(task_registry_size);\n530\t        reg!(scatter_fan_out_size);\n531\t        reg!(scatter_partial_responses);\n532\t        reg!(scatter_retries);\n533\t        reg!(rebalance_in_progress);\n534\t        reg!(rebalance_documents_migrated);\n535\t        reg!(rebalance_duration);\n536\t\n537\t        // ── §13.11 Multi-search metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 tenants, rest bucketed) ──\n538\t        let (\n539\t            multisearch_queries_per_batch,\n540\t            multisearch_batches_total,\n541\t            multisearch_partial_failures_total,\n542\t            multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total,\n543\t        ) = if config.multi_search.enabled {\n544\t            let q = Histogram::with_opts(\n545\t                HistogramOpts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch\", \"Number of queries in each multi-search batch\")\n546\t                    .buckets(vec![1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 10.0, 25.0, 50.0, 100.0]),\n547\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_queries_per_batch\");\n548\t            let b = Counter::with_opts(\n549\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_batches_total\", \"Total number of multi-search batches processed\"),\n550\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_batches_total\");\n551\t            let p = Counter::with_opts(\n552\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total\", \"Number of multi-search batches with at least one query failure\"),\n553\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_partial_failures_total\");\n554\t            let t = CounterVec::new(\n555\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total\", \"Session pin overrides triggered by multi-search tenant routing\"),\n556\t                &[\"tenant\"],\n557\t            ).expect(\"create multisearch_tenant_session_pin_override_total\");\n558\t            reg!(q); reg!(b); reg!(p); reg!(t);\n559\t            (Some(q), Some(b), Some(p), Some(t))\n560\t        } else {\n561\t            (None, None, None, None)\n562\t        };\n563\t\n564\t        // ── §13.12 Vector search metrics ──\n565\t        let (\n566\t            vector_search_over_fetched_total,\n567\t            vector_merge_strategy,\n568\t            vector_embedder_drift_total,\n569\t        ) = if config.vector_search.enabled {\n570\t            let o = Counter::with_opts(\n571\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_search_over_fetched_total\", \"Number of vector searches that over-fetched candidates\"),\n572\t            ).expect(\"create vector_search_over_fetched_total\");\n573\t            let m = CounterVec::new(\n574\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_merge_strategy\", \"Count of hybrid merge strategy selections\"),\n575\t                &[\"strategy\"],\n576\t            ).expect(\"create vector_merge_strategy\");\n577\t            let d = Counter::with_opts(\n578\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_vector_embedder_drift_total\", \"Number of embedder drift detections\"),\n579\t            ).expect(\"create vector_embedder_drift_total\");\n580\t            reg!(o); reg!(m); reg!(d);\n581\t            (Some(o), Some(m), Some(d))\n582\t        } else {\n583\t            (None, None, None)\n584\t        };\n585\t\n586\t        // ── §13.13 CDC metrics (cardinality cap: top 100 sinks, rest bucketed) ──\n587\t        let (\n588\t            cdc_events_published_total,\n589\t            cdc_lag_seconds,\n590\t            cdc_buffer_bytes,\n591\t            cdc_dropped_total,\n592\t            cdc_events_suppressed_total,\n593\t        ) = if config.cdc.enabled {\n594\t            let e = CounterVec::new(\n595\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_events_published_total\", \"Total CDC events published\"),\n596\t                &[\"sink\", \"index\"],\n597\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_events_published_total\");\n598\t            let l = GaugeVec::new(\n599\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_lag_seconds\", \"CDC delivery lag in seconds\"),\n600\t                &[\"sink\"],\n601\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_lag_seconds\");\n602\t            let b = GaugeVec::new(\n603\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_buffer_bytes\", \"CDC buffer size in bytes\"),\n604\t                &[\"sink\"],\n605\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_buffer_bytes\");\n606\t            let d = CounterVec::new(\n607\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_dropped_total\", \"CDC events dropped due to buffer overflow\"),\n608\t                &[\"sink\"],\n609\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_dropped_total\");\n610\t            let s = CounterVec::new(\n611\t                Opts::new(\"miroir_cdc_events_suppressed_total\", \"CDC events suppressed by origin deduplication\"),\n612\t                &[\"origin\"],\n613\t            ).expect(\"create cdc_events_suppressed_total\");\n614\t            reg!(e); reg!(l); reg!(b); reg!(d); reg!(s);\n615\t            (Some(e), Some(l), Some(b), Some(d), Some(s))\n616\t        } else TextEncoder::new();\n1061\t        let metric_families = self.registry.gather();\n1062\t        let mut buffer = Vec::new();\n1063\t        encoder.encode(&metric_families, &mut buffer)?;\n1064\t        Ok(String::from_utf8(buffer).map_err(|e| {\n1065\t            prometheus::Error::Msg(format!(\"failed to convert metrics to UTF-8: {}\", e))\n1066\t        })?)\n1067\t    }\n1068\t\n1069\t    pub fn admin_session_key_generated(&self) -> Gauge {\n1070\t        self.admin_session_key_generated.clone()\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    pub fn admin_session_revoked_total(&self) -> Counter {\n1074\t        self.admin_session_revoked_total.clone()\n1075\t    }\n1076\t}\n1077\t\n1078\t/// Generate a short request ID from UUIDv7.\n1079\t///\n1080\t/// UUIDv7 provides time-ordered unique IDs. We take the first 8 hex characters,\n1081\t/// hash them, and encode as hex for a short, URL-safe identifier.\n1082\tpub fn generate_request_id() -> String {\n1083\t    let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n1084\t    let uuid_str = uuid.simple().to_string();\n1085\t    // Take first 8 chars (enough entropy for uniqueness)\n1086\t    let prefix = &uuid_str[..8];\n1087\t\n1088\t    // Hash to get a consistent short representation\n1089\t    let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n1090\t    prefix.hash(&mut hasher);\n1091\t    let hash = hasher.finish();\n1092\t\n1093\t    // Encode as hex (16 chars = 64 bits)\n1094\t    format!(\"{:016x}\", hash)\n1095\t}\n1096\t\n1097\t/// Extension trait to add request ID extraction utilities.\n1098\tpub trait RequestIdExt {\n1099\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String>;\n1100\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str);\n1101\t}\n1102\t\n1103\timpl RequestIdExt for HeaderMap {\n1104\t    fn get_request_id(&self) -> Option<String> {\n1105\t        self.get(\"x-request-id\")\n1106\t            .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n1107\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n1108\t    }\n1109\t\n1110\t    fn set_request_id(&mut self, id: &str) {\n1111\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(id) {\n1112\t            self.insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1113\t        }\n1114\t    }\n1115\t}\n1116\t\n1117\t/// Guard that decrements the in-flight gauge when dropped.\n1118\t///\n1119\t/// This ensures that even if the handler panics, the in-flight count\n1120\t/// is accurately decremented.\n1121\tstruct InFlightGuard {\n1122\t    metrics: Metrics,\n1123\t    request_id: String,\n1124\t}\n1125\t\n1126\timpl InFlightGuard {\n1127\t    fn new(metrics: Metrics, request_id: String) -> Self {\n1128\t        metrics.requests_in_flight.inc();\n1129\t        tracing::trace!(\n1130\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1131\t            requests_in_flight = metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1132\t            \"request started\"\n1133\t        );\n1134\t        Self { metrics, request_id }\n1135\t    }\n1136\t}\n1137\t\n1138\timpl Drop for InFlightGuard {\n1139\t    fn drop(&mut self) {\n1140\t        self.metrics.requests_in_flight.dec();\n1141\t        tracing::trace!(\n1142\t            request_id = %self.request_id,\n1143\t            requests_in_flight = self.metrics.requests_in_flight.get(),\n1144\t            \"request completed\"\n1145\t        );\n1146\t    }\n1147\t}\n1148\t\n1149\t/// Extract the path template from the matched route.\n1150\t///\n1151\t/// Axum's MatchedPath extractor provides the route template (e.g., \"/indexes/{uid}/search\")\n1152\t/// instead of the actual path (e.g., \"/indexes/products/search\"), avoiding high-cardinality labels.\n1153\tfn extract_path_template(request: &Request) -> String {\n1154\t    request\n1155\t        .extensions()\n1156\t        .get::<axum::extract::MatchedPath>()\n1157\t        .map(|mp| mp.as_str())\n1158\t        .unwrap_or_else(|| request.uri().path())\n1159\t        .to_string()\n1160\t}\n1161\t\n1162\t/// Main middleware that combines request ID injection, structured logging, and Prometheus metrics.\n1163\t///\n1164\t/// IMPORTANT: This middleware must be applied AFTER request_id_middleware in the layer stack\n1165\t/// (i.e., its layer() call must come BEFORE request_id_middleware's layer() call).\n1166\t/// This ensures the request_id header is already set when this middleware runs.\n1167\tpub async fn telemetry_middleware(\n1168\t    State(telemetry): State<TelemetryState>,\n1169\t    mut req: Request,\n1170\t    next: Next,\n1171\t) -> Response {\n1172\t    let start = Instant::now();\n1173\t    let method = req.method().clone();\n1174\t    let path_template = extract_path_template(&req);\n1175\t    let metrics = telemetry.metrics.clone();\n1176\t    let pod_id = telemetry.pod_id.clone();\n1177\t\n1178\t    // Extract request ID from header (set by request_id_middleware)\n1179\t    // The header must already exist because request_id_middleware runs first.\n1180\t    let request_id = req\n1181\t        .headers()\n1182\t        .get_request_id()\n1183\t        .expect(\"request_id header must be set by request_id_middleware\");\n1184\t    req.headers_mut().set_request_id(&request_id);\n1185\t\n1186\t    // Create span for structured logging with pod_id included.\n1187\t    // Note: raw path is intentionally omitted to avoid logging index names\n1188\t    // (which may contain customer identifiers). Use path_template instead.\n1189\t    let span = info_span!(\n1190\t        \"request\",\n1191\t        request_id = %request_id,\n1192\t        pod_id = %pod_id,\n1193\t        method = %method,\n1194\t        path_template = %path_template,\n1195\t    );\n1196\t\n1197\t    let _guard = span.enter();\n1198\t\n1199\t    // Track in-flight requests\n1200\t    let in_flight = InFlightGuard::new(metrics.clone(), request_id.clone());\n1201\t\n1202\t    let response = next.run(req).await;\n1203\t\n1204\t    drop(in_flight);\n1205\t\n1206\t    let status = response.status();\n1207\t    let status_u16 = status.as_u16();\n1208\t    let duration = start.elapsed();\n1209\t\n1210\t    // Record Prometheus metrics\n1211\t    metrics\n1212\t        .request_duration\n1213\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1214\t        .observe(duration.as_secs_f64());\n1215\t    metrics\n1216\t        .requests_total\n1217\t        .with_label_values(&[method.as_str(), &path_template, &status_u16.to_string()])\n1218\t        .inc();\n1219\t\n1220\t    // Structured log entry (plan §10 shape)\n1221\t    // Base fields: timestamp (from tracing-subscriber), level, message, duration_ms\n1222\t    // Additional fields (index, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)\n1223\t    // are added by request handlers via the tracing span.\n1224\t    let message = format!(\"{} {}\", method, status);\n1225\t    if status.is_server_error() {\n1226\t        tracing::error!(\n1227\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1228\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1229\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1230\t            message = %message,\n1231\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1232\t            status = status_u16,\n1233\t            method = %method,\n1234\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1235\t        );\n1236\t    } else if status.is_client_error() {\n1237\t        tracing::warn!(\n1238\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1239\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1240\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1241\t            message = %message,\n1242\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1243\t            status = status_u16,\n1244\t            method = %method,\n1245\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1246\t        );\n1247\t    } else {\n1248\t        tracing::info!(\n1249\t            target: \"miroir.request\",\n1250\t            pod_id = %pod_id,\n1251\t            request_id = %request_id,\n1252\t            message = %message,\n1253\t            duration_ms = duration.as_millis() as u64,\n1254\t            status = status_u16,\n1255\t            method = %method,\n1256\t            path_template = %path_template,\n1257\t        );\n1258\t    }\n1259\t\n1260\t    // Ensure request ID is in response headers\n1261\t    let mut response = response;\n1262\t    if !response.headers().contains_key(\"x-request-id\") {\n1263\t        if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(&request_id) {\n1264\t            response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n1265\t        }\n1266\t    }\n1267\t\n1268\t    response\n1269\t}\n1270\t\n1271\t/// Create the metrics router for the :9090 server.\n1272\tpub fn metrics_router() -> Router<Metrics> {\n1273\t    Router::new().route(\"/metrics\", get(metrics_handler))\n1274\t}\n1275\t\n1276\t/// Handler that returns Prometheus metrics in text exposition format.\n1277\tasync fn metrics_handler(State(metrics): State<Metrics>) -> Response {\n1278\t    let body = match metrics.encode_metrics() {\n1279\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics,\n1280\t        Err(e) => {\n1281\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n1282\t            format!(\"# ERROR: failed to encode metrics: {}\\n\", e)\n1283\t        }\n1284\t    };\n1285\t\n1286\t    let mut response = Response::new(axum::body::Body::from(body));\n1287\t    response.headers_mut().insert(\n1288\t        \"content-type\",\n1289\t        HeaderValue::from_static(\"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\"),\n1290\t    );\n1291\t    response\n1292\t}\n1293\t\n1294\t/// Accessor methods for metrics that can be used by other parts of the application.\n1295\timpl Metrics {\n1296\t    // ── Structured logging, request IDs, and Prometheus metrics middleware.\n\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    http::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, StatusCode},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::{Response, IntoResponse},\n    Router,\n    routing::get,\n};\nuse miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse prometheus::{\n    Counter, CounterVec, Encoder, Gauge, GaugeVec, Histogram, HistogramOpts, HistogramVec, Opts,\n    Registry, TextEncoder,\n};\nuse tracing::info_span;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\nuse hex;\nuse std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n\n/// Request ID wrapper type for storing in axum Request extensions.\n///\n/// This is a newtype wrapper around the 8-character hex request ID,\n/// allowing handlers to extract it via `Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct RequestId(pub String);\n\nimpl RequestId {\n    /// Create a new RequestId from a UUIDv7.\n    ///\n    /// Hashes the full UUIDv7 to produce an 8-character hex ID that is unique\n    /// even for consecutive calls within the same millisecond.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let uuid = Uuid::now_v7();\n        let bytes = uuid.as_bytes();\n        // Hash the full UUID to ensure uniqueness even within the same millisecond\n        let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new();\n        hasher.write(bytes);\n        let hash = hasher.finish();\n        // Take first 8 hex chars of 64-bit hash (32 bits is sufficient entropy)\n        Self(format!(\"{:08x}\", hash as u32))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the inner request ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a RequestId from a string.\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()) {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session ID wrapper type for read-your-writes session pinning (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracted from the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stored in request extensions.\n/// Handlers can access it via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct SessionId(pub String);\n\nimpl Default for SessionId {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self(String::new())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionId {\n    /// Get the inner session ID string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.0\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a SessionId from a string.\n    ///\n    /// Accepts any non-empty string (client-provided UUID or identifier).\n    pub fn parse(s: String) -> Option<Self> {\n        if !s.is_empty() && s.len() <= 256 {\n            Some(Self(s))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn request_id_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Check for existing request ID in headers\n    let request_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| RequestId::parse(s.to_string()))\n        .unwrap_or_else(RequestId::new);\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    req.extensions_mut().insert(request_id.clone());\n\n    // Set X-Request-Id header on request (for telemetry_middleware to read)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        req.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    // Process the request\n    let mut response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    // Add X-Request-Id header to response (override if exists)\n    if let Ok(val) = HeaderValue::from_str(request_id.as_str()) {\n        response.headers_mut().insert(\"x-request-id\", val);\n    }\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Session pinning middleware (plan §13.6).\n///\n/// Extracts the `X-Miroir-Session` header and stores it in request extensions\n/// for handlers to access via `Request.extensions().get::<SessionId>()`.\npub async fn session_pinning_middleware(\n    mut req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    // Extract session ID from header if present\n    let session_id = req\n        .headers()\n        .get(\"x-miroir-session\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| SessionId::parse(s.to_string()));\n\n    // Store in request extensions for handler access\n    if let Some(sid) = session_id {\n        req.extensions_mut().insert(sid);\n    }\n\n    next.run(req).await\n}\n\n\n/// Telemetry state combining metrics and pod_id for middleware.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TelemetryState {\n    pub metrics: Metrics,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl TelemetryState {\n    pub fn new(metrics: Metrics) -> Self {\n        let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n        Self { metrics, pod_id }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Global metrics registry shared across all middleware instances.\npub struct Metrics {\n    registry: Registry,\n\n    // ── Request metrics ──\n    request_duration: HistogramVec,\n    requests_total: CounterVec,\n    requests_in_flight: Gauge,\n\n    // ── Node health metrics ──\n    node_healthy: GaugeVec,\n    node_request_duration: HistogramVec,\n
